title: quercus oxyloba
intro: Quercus oxyloba, also known as the Chiltepal or Mexican White Oak, is a deciduous tree native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its broad leaves and white flowers. The medicinal properties of Quercus oxyloba lie in its bark, leaves, and acorns, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Quercus oxyloba is traditionally used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, and its wood is also valued for its durability and resistance to decay.
